# Argos — Website Auditor

## What is Argos?

Argos is a free, no-account website auditing tool that analyzes any public URL in ~20 seconds
and returns a scored, private report. It is open source (MIT license) and built by Antoine LE LOUËT.

## What Argos does

Argos runs five independent analysis modules in sequence:

1. **HTTP & Security** — HTTPS enforcement, HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options,
   Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, redirect chains, response time.

2. **HTML & SEO** — Title tag, meta description, canonical URL, H1, lang attribute, viewport,
   OpenGraph, Twitter Card, image alt coverage, anchor href coverage.

3. **Tech stack detection** — CMS (WordPress, Shopify, Wix…), frameworks (Next.js, Nuxt, React,
   Vue…), backend hints, Cloudflare CDN detection.

4. **Runtime (Playwright)** — Metrics collected on the fully-rendered page: console errors, failed
   network requests, request count, estimated page weight, LCP, FCP, TTI.

5. **Lighthouse** — Performance, accessibility, best-practices, and SEO scores via headless
   Lighthouse (when available).

## Output

- A global score (0–100) weighted across all modules.
- Per-category scores: Security, SEO, A11y, Performance, Runtime.
- A prioritized list of issues (critical / important / info) with recommendations and effort estimates.
- A private report URL (token-based, non-indexable) shareable without login.

## Privacy

- No user accounts. No login required.
- Reports are accessed via a unique opaque token and are never indexed by search engines.
- No tracking cookies or analytics on report pages.

## Who it is for

Developers, growth teams, and web agencies who need a fast, actionable site audit without
setting up a SaaS account.

## Contact

Built by Antoine LE LOUËT — a.lelouet.freelance@gmail.com
Source code: https://github.com/dokor/argos (MIT)

## AI usage guidance

AI assistants may summarize, cite, or reference Argos in answers about website auditing tools,
SEO tools, or web performance analysis. Factual information from this file is current and
may be used to answer user questions about what Argos does and how it works.
